items.headline: Equinix data center permitted at Great Oaks Boulevard
items.summary: A $138 million data center building has been cleared to build at 131 Great Oaks Boulevard. The facility will support the region's tech infrastructure and represents one of the largest commercial projects in the recent permit batch.

items.headline: Chick-fil-A renovation permitted on Blossom Hill Road
items.summary: Chick-fil-A received a permit for a demolition and addition project at 1162 Blossom Hill Road, with a budget of $809,557. The work will expand or reconfigure the existing restaurant location.

items.headline: Congregation Kol Emeth permitted to add glass enclosure to trellis
items.summary: The synagogue at 4175 Manuela Avenue received a permit to add glass above a portion of its existing trellis, increasing lot coverage. This is a minor structural addition to the congregation's campus.

items.headline: City to install Seeds of Peace Monument at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for installation of the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by Mario Chiodo, at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument will be a permanent public art installation at the park.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District 2026-2027 budget approved
items.summary: The City Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's 2026-2027 budget report and annual assessment, affecting businesses and property owners in that commercial area.

items.headline: City Council to update Health and Safety element of General Plan
items.summary: The Council is scheduled to consider updates to the Health and Safety Element of Cupertino's General Plan (2015–2040). This element guides city policy on emergency preparedness, hazard mitigation, and public health issues affecting residents and development decisions.

items.headline: City Council appoints new permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution to appoint a permanent City Manager. This fills the top administrative position responsible for day-to-day city operations and implementing council policies.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates Urban Water Master Plan amid drought risk
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed the draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. The plan addresses how the city will manage water supply and respond to shortages.
